data.title: Bitcoin's Plunge Leaves MicroStrategy with $3.9B Loss
data.description:
MicroStrategy CEO Michael Saylor's strategy of investing in Bitcoin has taken a significant hit in recent weeks, with the company's Bitcoin holdings now valued at a loss of $3,868,168,653. This represents a substantial decrease from the peak value of the company's Bitcoin portfolio, which was reached in early 2021.

MicroStrategy, a business intelligence firm, has been a prominent advocate for Bitcoin, with Saylor himself being one of the most vocal proponents of the cryptocurrency. The company has invested heavily in Bitcoin, with its holdings currently standing at around 129,218 Bitcoins, valued at approximately $6.1 billion at the time of writing.

The recent decline in the value of Bitcoin has had a significant impact on MicroStrategy's balance sheet. The company's Bitcoin holdings are now valued at a loss of $3,868,168,653, representing a significant decrease from the peak value of the company's Bitcoin portfolio, which was reached in early 2021.

Despite the recent losses, Saylor remains optimistic about the long-term prospects of Bitcoin. In a recent interview, he stated that he believes that Bitcoin will continue to appreciate in value over the long term, and that the recent decline is simply a temporary setback.

MicroStrategy's investment in Bitcoin has been a controversial topic in the business world. While some have praised the company's forward-thinking approach to investing, others have criticized the company's heavy reliance on a single asset class. However, Saylor has remained steadfast in his support of Bitcoin, and has stated that the company will continue to invest in the cryptocurrency.

As the value of Bitcoin continues to fluctuate, investors and businesses alike will be watching MicroStrategy's Bitcoin holdings closely. The company's investment in the cryptocurrency has been a high-profile example of the potential risks and rewards of investing in digital assets, and its future performance will be a key indicator of the broader cryptocurrency market's health.

data.title: SEC: Meme Coins Like Dogecoin Not Securities
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has recently announced that meme coins, such as Dogecoin and Shiba Inu, will not be classified as securities. This decision has significant implications for the future of these cryptocurrencies and the broader crypto industry.

Meme coins, like Dogecoin and Shiba Inu, started as jokes but have since gained substantial popularity and recognition. Dogecoin, for instance, was inspired by a popular social media meme featuring a Shiba Inu dog. Initially created for fun, these coins have since amassed a strong following, particularly after receiving support from influential figures like Elon Musk. This support has led to the emergence of other meme coins, such as Shiba Inu, which aim to replicate Dogecoin's success.

The SEC's ruling clarifies the regulatory status of meme coins. The commission has determined that these coins do not meet the criteria for classification as securities. This means that meme coins will not be subject to the same regulations as stocks or bonds. To be considered a security, an asset must derive profits from the work of others. Since meme coins do not meet this requirement, they are not classified as securities.

While this decision may seem like good news for crypto investors, it does not necessarily make meme coins safer or more stable investments. The SEC's ruling highlights the fact that meme coins are more dependent on market sentiment and social media hype than on long-term value or use cases. This means that their prices can fluctuate rapidly, and their value is largely driven by trends rather than practical applications.

For investors considering meme coins, the SEC's decision does not change the fact that these investments are high-risk and unpredictable. Prices can shift dramatically based on social media trends or celebrity endorsements, but they can also decline rapidly. It is crucial for investors to be aware of the risks associated with meme coins and to understand that their value is difficult to predict.

The future of meme coins remains uncertain. While the SEC's decision may provide some relief from additional regulation, it does not guarantee long-term success. Meme coins are likely to continue attracting investors seeking immediate gains, but their long-term potential is still unpredictable. Without real use cases or strong backing, these coins may struggle to maintain their value over time.

In conclusion, the SEC's decision to not classify meme coins as securities has

data.title: Dell's AI Boom Can't Stop a 7% Stock Drop - Along With Earnings Miss and Accounting Bombshell
data.description:
Dell Technologies (NYSE: DELL) has been riding the wave of artificial intelligence (AI) innovation, but its stock price has taken a hit despite the reported AI boom. The company's shares dropped by around 3% on Monday, February 27, 2025, despite announcing strong fiscal 2025 fourth-quarter and full-year results. The stock drop can be attributed to several factors, including supply chain issues, a choppy short-term outlook, and potential deal uncertainty. Additionally, dell reported an earnings miss and an accounting bombshell, which further impacted investor sentiment.



Dell's AI boom has been driven by its end-to-end AI portfolio, spanning client devices, servers, storage, data protection, and networking. The company has expanded this portfolio with new offers purpose-built to meet increasing AI demands, such as AI PCs with Copilot+ features, storage advancements like PowerScale F910, and networking innovations like PowerSwitch Z9864F-ON. Dell has also collaborated with leading ecosystem partners like Hugging Face and Meta to simplify the deployment of AI applications and accelerate time to value for customers.



However, Dell's stock drop highlights the challenges the company faces in managing investor expectations and communicating its strategic vision effectively. The earnings miss and accounting bombshell further exacerbated investor concerns, as the company reported an EPS of $2.15, missing analysts' expectations of $2.51, and announced a $10 billion write-down related to its acquisition of EMC in 2016.



Despite these setbacks, Dell's strong financial performance and commitment to shareholder return suggest that the company's fundamentals remain robust. The company reported a full-year revenue of $95.6 billion, up 8% year over year, and a record full-year diluted EPS of $6.38, up 39% year over year. Additionally, Dell announced a cash dividend increase of 18% and a $10 billion increase in its share repurchase authorization, demonstrating its focus on long-term value creation.



In conclusion, Dell's AI boom has not been enough to prevent a 7% stock drop, as the company grapples with supply chain issues, a choppy short-term outlook, and potential deal uncertainty. The earnings miss and accounting bombshell have further impacted investor sentiment, but Dell's strong financial performance and commitment to shareholder return suggest that the company's fundamentals remain solid. As Dell continues to drive AI innovation and execute on its strategic vision, investors should closely monitor the company's progress and consider the potential long-term implications of these recent developments.

data.title: Trump's Warning to Zelenskyy: A Tense White House Meeting and Its Geopolitical Implications
data.description:
The White House meeting between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday, March 1, 2025, turned tense as Trump warned Zelenskyy that he was "gambling with World War III." The heated exchange, which was caught on camera, has raised concerns about the future of the Russia-Ukraine conflict and the broader geopolitical landscape.

The meeting was scheduled to sign a deal granting the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth and critical minerals, which Trump had previously described as a "huge economic development project." However, the tense exchange between the two leaders led to the cancellation of the signing ceremony and Zelenskyy's early departure from the White House.

Trump's warning to Zelenskyy has significant implications for the ongoing peace negotiations and the future of the Russia-Ukraine conflict. By threatening to pull out of the peace process if Zelenskyy doesn't make a deal with Russia, Trump could be putting pressure on Ukraine to accept terms that may not be in its best interest. This could lead to a situation where Ukraine feels compelled to make concessions that could jeopardize its sovereignty and territorial integrity.

Moreover, Trump's statement that Ukraine doesn't have the cards and is in a weak position could embolden Russia to make further demands or even escalate the conflict. This could lead to a more precarious security situation for Ukraine, potentially putting more lives at risk.

The cancellation of the news conference and Zelenskyy's early departure from the White House could also have broader geopolitical implications. The breakdown in relations between Trump and Zelenskyy could strain U.S.-Ukraine relations, potentially leading to a decrease in U.S. support for Ukraine, both militarily and economically. This could further weaken Ukraine's position in the conflict and undermine its ability to resist Russian aggression.

Furthermore, the public display of tension between Trump and Zelenskyy could impact international perceptions of the conflict and the U.S.'s role in it. Other countries may be less likely to support Ukraine if they perceive the U.S. as not fully committed to the peace process or Ukraine's security. This could potentially undermine the U.S.'s credibility as a global leader and embolden its adversaries.

In conclusion, the tense exchange between Trump and Zelenskyy has the potential to significantly impact the peace negotiations, Ukraine's security, U.S.-Ukraine relations, international perceptions, and the potential for further escalation of the conflict. It is crucial for all parties involved to approach the negotiations with caution and a commitment to finding a peaceful resolution that respects Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity.

data.title: S&P 500 Roams All-Time Highs But Bearish Sentiment Is Roaring In The Market...Is It Time To Panic?
data.description:
The panic among stock market investors is intensifying, even as the S&P 500 continues to hover near its all-time highs.

According to a report released by Bespoke Investment Group on Thursday, "What happened to sentiment? Everywhere you look, fear has set into the collective mood." The firm cited multiple indicators reflecting a rise in market panic, showing a clear deterioration in investor confidence.

This sentiment has spread across various levels, including economic uncertainty indicators. The Economic Policy Uncertainty Index, developed by economists Scott Baker, Nick Bloom, and Steven J. Davis, has surpassed levels seen during the worst of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Earlier data from the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index revealed that the index fell to its lowest level since November 2023, further exacerbating market concerns. On Tuesday, the Consumer Confidence Index released by the Conference Board also dropped sharply by 7 points to 98.3, its lowest level in eight months, adding to investor unease.

Bespoke analysts noted that while economic and consumer data have worsened, the most evident panic is still reflected in the stock market.

Among widely followed market sentiment indicators, CNN's Fear & Greed Index dropped to 21 early Thursday, indicating a state of "extreme fear." This index aggregates factors such as market momentum, market breadth, options trading activity, high-yield bond markets, and demand for safe-haven assets.

Additionally, the weekly Investor Sentiment Survey by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) showed a significant rise in bearish sentiment. The report stated, "The bears are out in full force." Data revealed that bullish sentiment plummeted from 29.2% last week to 19.4%, hitting its lowest level since March 2023.

More notably, bearish sentiment surged even more dramatically. AAII data showed that bearish sentiment jumped from 40.5% last week to over 60%, marking the largest weekly increase since August 2019.

Investors generally believe that extreme panic often occurs near market bottoms. However, what makes this wave of market panic unique is that despite the sharp rise in fear, the S&P 500, as of Wednesday's close, was only down 3.1% from its all-time high set on February 19.

Bespoke analysts wrote, "It takes a lot less to strike fear into investors than it has in the past. If there is one word to describe the state of investor sentiment right now, complacent it is not."

Although market data remains relatively robust, the rapid collapse of investor confidence could further exacerbate market volatility. In the current macroeconomic environment, the future direction of the market will depend on whether investor sentiment stabilizes and whether subsequent economic data can provide new support.

data.title: Bitcoin Plummet 16%! Buy the Dip or Run?
data.description:
Over the past week, Bitcoin has suffered a significant drop, plunging 16%—its largest weekly decline since the collapse of the FTX crypto exchange in November 2022. As of publication, Bitcoin is trading near $80,000, down over 25% from its peak of $110,000. 

Other cryptocurrencies have suffered even heavier losses, with Ethereum tumbling from $4,200 to $2,000, effectively halving in value.

Why Is Bitcoin Dropping?   

Market analysts widely attribute Bitcoin's recent decline to growing global risk aversion triggered by two key factors: Trump's continued tariff hike rhetoric and the Federal Reserve's hawkish stance.    Investors have been selling off risky assets, including equities and cryptocurrencies.

The idea of Bitcoin as an inflation hedge and safe-haven asset was thoroughly discredited during the 2022 bear market. Today, Bitcoin moves in close correlation with U.S. equities, particularly tech stocks. Given that the stock market has also seen a sharp sell-off recently, Bitcoin's decline reflects broader market sentiment.

When Trump first took office, he appointed officials favorable to cryptocurrency, fueling excitement that helped Bitcoin break above $100,000. However, the market's bullish sentiment has since faded, and the Trump administration has yet to introduce any concrete policies to boost crypto investment. Worse yet, Trump and his wife have personally contributed to investor skepticism.    Trump's own TRUMP memecoin has lost 50% of its value since its launch, while MELANIA, a token linked to his wife, has plummeted 90%. These losses have further eroded investor confidence.

Another major factor contributing to the sell-off is the recent wave of high-profile cryptocurrency thefts. The most significant was the February 21 hack of Bybit, a crypto exchange, in which hackers stole approximately $1.5 billion worth of Ethereum—the largest theft in the industry's history.

Data from crypto analytics firm SoSoValue shows that since February 2025, Bitcoin spot ETFs have seen net outflows of $3.4 billion. On February 25, when Bitcoin broke below $90,000, ETF outflows hit a record $1.13 billion in a single day—the highest since Bitcoin ETFs were introduced. Historical trends suggest that significant ETF inflows or outflows often signal major price turning points for Bitcoin.

Buy the Dip or Escape the Crash?   

Some analysts warn that Bitcoin's downtrend is far from over.

Bloomberg strategist Mark Cudmore cautioned, "The real panic may be ahead of us still. Bitcoin always has another 70%+ crash in its future, by design. The $72K-$74K range appears to be a critical technical level that could trigger the next crypto winter."

Investors are now closely watching to see how much further Bitcoin could fall. Ruslan Lienkha, Chief of Markets at crypto platform YouHodler, noted that technical analysis suggests support around $70,000 but warned against assuming the worst-case scenario will materialize.

On the other hand, crypto bulls argue that this is a prime buying opportunity.

Joel Kruger, a market strategist at lmax, sees a potential turning point in March: "There will be an opportunity for Bitcoin to shine if global markets experience intensified stress. If Bitcoin drops into the $70,000 to $75,000 range, it may start demonstrating its true store-of-value properties rather than moving in lockstep with stocks and risk sentiment."

He added, "Within the darkness of the pullbacks, there will be a silver lining.    Bitcoin's resilience through this downturn will help solidify its maturity as an asset."

data.title: Trump's Firm Files Metaverse NFT Trademark
data.description:
DTTM Operations, a company managing U.S. President Donald Trump's intellectual property rights, has filed a trademark with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) that hints at potential plans for non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and a metaverse platform. The filing lists various products and services related to blockchain technology, including accessing NFTs, managing cryptocurrency transactions, virtual reality software and hardware, and game software featuring crypto tokens for use in online virtual worlds.

The filing status indicates that the application has been accepted by the office and is awaiting an examiner. While the nature of the filing does not directly reveal the specifics of the platform or its launch timeline, it does mention the inclusion of various collectibles of Trump. The filing states, "Computer services, namely, creating an online virtual environment for exchange of digital collectibles featuring images, sound, videos, and texts of Donald J. Trump."

Trump has previously shown interest in the crypto space, launching his own TRUMP memecoin and expressing support for making bitcoin the reserve currency of the U.S. He has also released his own NFTs, some of which offered owners access to exclusive experiences such as Trump sneakers, Trump cocktails, and dinner at Trump National golf Club in jupiter, Florida, with Trump himself.

If this metaverse platform materializes, it could potentially revitalize metaverse-related tokens such as Sandbox (SAND) and Decentraland (MANA), which have been struggling since the 2021 hype around the metaverse faded. However, it is essential to note that filing a trademark does not guarantee the launch of a product. The success of this venture will depend on how Trump's firm decides to launch the platform and whether it gets rolled out at all.

data.title: Frontdoor's Q4 Surprise: Stock Plunges 3.09%
C3.ai: A Deep Dive into the Stock's 2025 Decline
data.description:
C3.ai, Inc. (AI), a leading enterprise AI software company, has experienced a significant decline in its stock price in 2025, with a year-to-date loss of 35.77% as of March 1, 2025. This article explores the primary factors contributing to this decline and compares them to the company's performance in previous years.



1. Analyst Price Targets and Sentiment: Multiple analysts have lowered their price targets for c3.ai's stock, indicating a decrease in their confidence in the company's future performance. For example:
* Northland Securities lowered their price target from $35 to $45.
* DA Davidson lowered their price target from $25 to $40.
* Citizens JMP lowered their price target from $50 to $55.
* KeyBanc lowered their price target from $21 to $29.
* piper sandler lowered their price target from $28 to $42.
* morgan stanley lowered their price target from $30 to $32.
* Canaccord lowered their price target from $30 to $40.

These lowered price targets reflect a shift in analyst sentiment, which may contribute to the stock's decline.

2. Financial Outlook and Profitability Concerns: C3.ai's management no longer anticipates generating positive free cash flow for fiscal year 2025. Additionally, the company guided fiscal year 2025 operating losses to $120.0 million at the midpoint, which is $10 million wider than their previous outlook. These financial concerns may contribute to the stock price decline.
3. Comparison with Previous Years: In the fiscal third quarter of 2025, C3.ai reported revenue growth of 26% year-over-year, with total revenue reaching $98.8 million. While this growth is positive, it may not be enough to offset the financial concerns and lowered price targets mentioned above. In previous years, C3.ai has experienced consistent revenue growth, but the company's stock price has not shown a corresponding increase. For instance, in the fiscal first quarter of 2024, C3.ai reported revenue growth of 21% year-over-year, but the stock price did not significantly improve.



In conclusion, the primary factors contributing to C3.ai's significant stock price decline in 2025 are the lowered price targets by analysts, financial outlook and profitability concerns, and the company's performance compared to previous years. While C3.ai has experienced revenue growth, the stock price has not reflected this growth, indicating that investors may be more focused on the company's financial outlook and profitability concerns.

Important note: Investors are reminded to do their due diligence and not rely on the information provided as financial advice. Consider this article as supplementing your required research. Please always apply independent thinking.

data.title: Knight-Swift Soars 2.65% Ahead of Q4 Earnings
SoundHound AI's CEO On AI Agents Transforming The World: 'This Is An Inevitable Evolution'
data.description:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, soundhound AI's CEO, Keyvan Mohajer, envisions a future where AI agents become ubiquitous, transforming industries and everyday life. As the company expands its reach across various sectors, Mohajer shares his perspective on the transformative potential of AI agents and the opportunities they present for investors.



Mohajer believes that the world is on the cusp of an "agentic AI revolution," where AI agents will take on more complex tasks, freeing up human resources for higher-level activities and driving optimization. This shift, he argues, is an inevitable evolution in AI functionality for customers, adding a new dimension to SoundHound's existing capabilities and impact.

One of the innovations SoundHound is most excited about is its newly unveiled voice commerce ecosystem. This end-to-end experience allows users of products powered by the company's voice assistant to seamlessly transact with businesses using natural speech. This ecosystem benefits everyone: it enhances convenience and safety for users, drives new leads for merchants, and creates a monetizable opportunity for SoundHound, with revenue-sharing potential for OEMs.

Beyond voice commerce, SoundHound is also at the forefront of the agentic AI revolution. As a pure-play AI software company with deep expertise in voice and conversational AI and enterprise-grade solutions, SoundHound is uniquely positioned to lead this next wave of innovation. This shift could have a profound impact on various industries, as AI agents take on more complex tasks, freeing up human resources for higher-level activities and driving optimization.

SoundHound is actively exploring new verticals, such as retail, healthcare, financial services, and energy, in addition to its strong partnerships in automotive and quick-service restaurants. By providing branded AI assistants for products and AI customer service for businesses, SoundHound can extend its solutions to benefit both customers and employees across these sectors. This expansion could lead to improved customer experiences, increased efficiency, and cost savings for businesses in these industries.



Mohajer's vision for AI agents in 2025 aligns with the predictions made by tech media and industry experts, who anticipate that AI agents will transform the way work is done, impacting every facet of our lives, personal and professional. As AI agents become more prevalent, investors should be aware of the potential risks and opportunities associated with this rapidly evolving market.

Opportunities abound for investors who identify promising companies like soundhound ai, as the expanding market for AI agents presents a significant growth area in 2025. However, investors should also be aware of potential challenges, such as market saturation, technological hurdles, regulatory concerns, and economic downturns. By carefully evaluating the market landscape and the competitive position of companies like SoundHound AI, investors can make informed decisions about their investments in the AI agent space.

In conclusion, SoundHound AI's CEO, Keyvan Mohajer, envisions a future where AI agents transform industries and everyday life, driving optimization and enhancing user experiences. As the company expands its reach across various sectors, investors should consider the potential risks and opportunities associated with this rapidly evolving market. By carefully evaluating the market landscape and the competitive position of companies like SoundHound AI, investors can make informed decisions about their investments in the AI agent space.

data.title: Shares Plummet: Earnings Lockout Stuns Investors
70% Chance of June Fed Cut as PCE Matches Expectations
data.description:
The core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, which excludes food and energy, rose 0.3% in January from the previous month. On a year-over-year basis, it increased 2.6%, marking the smallest annual gain since early 2021, according to newly released data.

Consumer Spending Drops Sharply

Inflation-adjusted consumer spending fell 0.5%, registering the largest monthly decline in nearly four years. The drop followed a strong holiday season and was exacerbated by severe winter weather. A significant decline in motor vehicle purchases drove the weakness, while spending on services also slowed.

Income Growth Surprises, but Savings Surge

Elsewhere in the report, personal income saw a much stronger-than-expected increase of 0.9% for the month, far exceeding the forecasted 0.4% gain. However, higher income did not translate into more spending, as personal consumption fell 0.2%, contrary to expectations of a 0.1% increase.

The personal savings rate spiked to 4.6%, indicating that consumers are choosing to save rather than spend, despite rising incomes.

Market Implications

Looking ahead, it remains uncertain how ongoing price pressures and aggressive policy shifts, including new tariffs on imports, will impact consumer behavior. While utility spending increased due to cold weather, overall services spending remained sluggish in January.

Following the report, futures markets increased the probability of a June Fed rate cut, with the CME FedWatch Tool now showing odds just above 70%. Markets currently anticipate two rate cuts by year-end.

Analyst Views

According to Bloomberg Economics strategists Stuart Paul, Eliza Winger, and Estelle Ou, labor income growth has been cooling, which could be a headwind for future personal income growth. However, inflation continues to trend toward the Fed's 2% target, and weaker consumer spending further supports the case for a rate cut.

The analysts expect February's payroll data (due March 7) to be weak, reinforcing the economic trajectory toward an eventual Fed rate cut once the current rate pause ends.

data.title: NVIDIA's Shares Plunged Yesterday, But It Could Be A Golden Buying Opportunity for 'Believers'
data.description:
NVIDIA's strong growth momentum in the AI sector and its relatively low valuation after a significant drop may present investors with an unmissable entry opportunity.

On Thursday, nvidia experienced a sharp decline of 8.48%, marking the largest daily drop since the DeepSeek impact, with both the Nasdaq and NVIDIA falling significantly. This caused NVIDIA's market capitalization to fall below the $3 trillion mark, dragging the chip index down by over 6%.

From a valuation perspective, NVIDIA's current forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio is 27 times, which is lower than Apple's 32 times forward P/E ratio, despite both being members of the $3 trillion market cap club. However, NVIDIA's growth rate is 20 times that of Apple—Apple's latest quarterly revenue grew by only 4%.

Therefore, in some analysts' views, this comparison makes NVIDIA's valuation appear relatively cheap. They pointed out that NVIDIA's current price corresponds to a 2026 P/E ratio of 20 times, making it almost the cheapest among the seven major U.S. tech giants. The low valuation reflects the current crowdedness of holdings, debates over the long-term sustainability of the business, and short-term noise disturbances. These factors might align well with the interests of long-term value investors.

In terms of performance, NVIDIA continues to shine. According to NVIDIA's latest quarterly earnings report, the company's revenue increased by 78% year-over-year to $39.3 billion, surpassing Wall Street ′s expectation of $38.1 billion. Its data center business revenue nearly doubled to $35.6 billion, primarily driven by strong sales of AI chips. Additionally, the company's revenue guidance for the current quarter is $43 billion, higher than the analyst consensus of $42.1 billion.

However, investor caution is not without reason. Overnight, Trump announced the imposition of tariffs on Canada, Mexico, and other countries starting March 4, which caused NVIDIA's stock to drop significantly after the market opened. Analysts believe that tariff policies pose the biggest risk for NVIDIA this year, especially potential tariffs on chips that could affect products manufactured by NVIDIA in Asia.

 Nonetheless, analysts suggest that despite policy uncertainties, investors should focus more on NVIDIA's fundamental prospects. With the successful launch of Blackwell and the growing demand for AI-driven AI agents, inference capabilities, and multimodal models, NVIDIA's performance in the coming quarters is expected to remain strong.

 NVIDIA's absolute leading position in the AI chip sector, the supply-demand imbalance in the market, a clear product iteration roadmap, and relatively reasonable valuation multiples provide a solid foundation for its continued growth. For long-term investors, short-term stock price fluctuations may offer a good entry point.

Meanwhile, Cardano's price dropped by 23% this week, with on-chain data suggesting weak network demand and potential further decline. pi coin, on the other hand, experienced extreme volatility, surging over 300% after the Pi Network Open Mainnet launch before falling 11.1% to $2.49 as of February 28, 2025.

In another development, Bitdeer purchased a 101 MW natural gas plant in Alberta for $21.7 million, with plans to utilize it for Bitcoin mining and potential expansion to 1 GW. The company also got approval to construct a 99 MW interconnection grid in collaboration with the Alberta Electric System Operator, valued at approximately $30 million (or $300,000 per MW), to support increased data center capacity dedicated to Bitcoin mining.

Cardano's price encountered resistance at its descending trendline, leading to a 15% decline after February 21. By Monday, it had dropped below its weekly support at $0.74 and continued falling an additional 6% over the next three days. As of February 28, Cardano is trading at $0.59, reflecting a decline of 6.93%.

data.title: Kalamazoo's $10M Guaranteed Income Pilot: A Cash Prescription for New Moms
data.description:
In a pioneering move, a U.S. city has launched a guaranteed income pilot program that will distribute millions of dollars annually to families living below the poverty line. The "Rx Kids" program, based in Kalamazoo, Michigan, aims to provide financial assistance to pregnant and nursing mothers during the early stages of their babies' lives.

The program, billed as the first-ever cash prescription initiative in Kalamazoo, has allocated $10 million to help families in need. Selected participants will receive a one-time payment of $1,500 after 16 weeks of pregnancy and $500 every month for the first 12 months of the baby's life. Enrollment for the program began on February 12th, and monthly payments will cease after the baby's first birthday.

Unlike traditional welfare programs, the rx kids initiative has no income requirements or strings attached. Families are free to spend the money as they see fit, promoting financial security and empowering them to make decisions that best suit their needs.

The Kalamazoo guaranteed income pilot program has raised $10 million so far, with the expectation that this amount will fund the project for two years. The program is a collaboration between the Michigan State University Pediatric Public Health Initiative and Poverty Solutions at the University of Michigan, administered by Give Directly, a nonprofit organization.

Similar programs have been launched in other cities, such as Flint, northeast of Kalamazoo, with the goal of improving maternal and infant health outcomes and bolstering family financial security. Rx Kids director, Mona Hanna, expressed her enthusiasm for the program's potential impact on communities across the state.

The guaranteed income pilot program in Kalamazoo represents a significant step towards addressing poverty and promoting financial stability for families in need. By providing cash assistance with no strings attached, the program empowers recipients to make decisions that best support their families and improve their overall well-being.
